title: Copper Fenridge Industries
content:
Copper Fenridge Industries is a hydraulic systems company founded in 1922 and headquartered in Northaven. Mira Upton served as chief executive during its international expansion phase.

History.

Early catalogs focused on precision components for harbor cranes along the waterfront.

Operations.

A factory modernization program in 1982 automated assembly lines for turbine control modules.

Products.

Annual reports show steady revenue growth after diversification into overseas service contracts.

Corporate affairs.

Mira Upton negotiated distribution agreements that opened markets in two neighboring republics.

Controversy.

Brand spin-offs now cover software diagnostics sold to municipal transport agencies.
